review_summary: The transcript is sufficient to generate 3 reinforcement questions about model introspection, evaluation mode, inspecting hidden states and attention outputs, and one code-style API check.

repaired_question_bank.question: What configuration is needed if you want a Hugging Face model to return internal hidden states and attention tensors?
choices: Load it with output_attentions=True and output_hidden_states=True
Only switch the tokenizer to batch mode
Use Trainer instead of a direct model call
repaired_question_bank.explanation: Those two flags tell the model to include internal activations and attention weights in its outputs so they can be inspected.

repaired_question_bank.question: Why is model.eval() called before inspecting hidden states and attention?
choices: Because the goal is evaluation or analysis rather than training, so training-time behavior is disabled
Because eval mode changes the tokenizer vocabulary
Because eval mode forces the model to return logits only
Because eval mode adds more attention heads for visualization
repaired_question_bank.explanation: Evaluation mode is the standard PyTorch setting when you want to inspect or run a pretrained model without training-time behavior such as dropout affecting the output.

transcript_quotes: set the model to be in eval mode
we do not actually want to backpropagate

repaired_question_bank.question: Why are output_hidden_states=True and output_attentions=True useful in this code?
choices: They make the model return internal hidden-state and attention information for inspection
They automatically fine-tune the model on the input batch
They force the model into training mode so dropout is enabled
They replace token ids with decoded strings in the output
repaired_question_bank.explanation: Those flags request intermediate representations from the forward pass so the model can be inspected instead of treated as a black box.

code_block.snippet:
from transformers import AutoModel
model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(
    "bert-base-uncased",
    output_hidden_states=True,
    output_attentions=True,
)

"Why is model.eval() called before inspecting hidden states and attention" asks for applied understanding rather than pure recall, so a medium prior fits.

"Why are output_hidden_states=True and output_attentions=True useful in this code" asks for applied understanding rather than pure recall, so a medium prior fits. The code context nudges the prior slightly upward.

item_phase_map.phase_rationale: "What configuration is needed if you want a Hugging Face model to return internal hidden states and attention tensors" is more useful for contextual reinforcement than for initial placement.
"Why is model.eval() called before inspecting hidden states and attention" naturally belongs in transfer-style evaluation.
"Why are output_hidden_states=True and output_attentions=True useful in this code" is well suited to checking whether the learner can reason through code rather than just recall facts.
